items.question: A company wants to expand from one AWS Region into a second AWS Region. What does the company need to do to start supporting the new Region?

options.A: Contact an AWS Account Manager to sign a new contract
options.B: Move an Availability Zone to the new Region
options.C: Begin deploying resources in the second Region
options.D: Download the AWS Management Console for the new Region

items.question: A company wants to set up a highly available workload in AWS with a disaster recovery plan that will allow the company to recover in case of a regional service interruption. Which configuration will meet these requirements?

options.A: Run on two Availability Zones in one AWS Region, using the additional Availability Zones in the AWS Region for the disaster recovery site.
options.B: Run on two Availability Zones in one AWS Region, using another AWS Region for the disaster recovery site.
options.C: Run on two Availability Zones in one AWS Region, using a local AWS Region for the disaster recovery site.
options.D: Run across two AWS Regions, using a third AWS Region for the disaster recovery site.

items.question: How can a user achieve high availability for a web application hosted on AWS?

options.A: Use a Classic Load Balancer across multiple AWS Regions.
options.B: Use an Application Load Balancer across multiple Availability Zones in one AWS Region.
options.C: Set up automatic scaling and load balancing with another application instance running on premises.
options.D: Use the AWS Region with the highest number of Availability Zones.
